title: Within-sequence candidate-lap reranking
summary: This target is fundamentally about choosing the imminent stop lap within a short driver-race-stint sequence, not just classifying independent rows.
rationale: This target is fundamentally about choosing the imminent stop lap within a short driver-race-stint sequence, not just classifying independent rows. Learning-to-rank methods are designed to order items inside a group and can complement a standard ROC-AUC classifier by improving local ordering near true stop windows.
implementation_hint: Form groups by driver-race-stint and restrict training to candidate windows, e.g. laps with moderate base hazard or late TyreLife. Train a LightGBM/XGBoost ranker to order candidate laps within each group using the existing handcrafted urgency features plus race-lap context; blend its rank-normalized output with the base binary model.
expected_effect: Should convert broad hazard signals into better lap-to-lap discrimination, especially when several adjacent laps all look plausible to a pure classifier.
risk: Ranking objectives optimize within-group ordering, not probability calibration; poor candidate-window construction can drop recall. Keep the classifier as the backbone and use the ranker as a local refiner.
